response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned web and backend engineer with over 15 years of experience, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role on multiple critical dimensions. The most absolute disqualifier is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency — native-level Japanese is a hard requirement and there is no indication of any Japanese ability. Beyond that, the candidate's technical background is overwhelmingly web-focused (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB) with no evidence of expertise in embedded networking security (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, tamper detection), L2/L3 networking, Linux kernel modification, or switch firmware development — all of which are core to the role.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across multiple industries and tech stacks
Some C/C++ background from National Instruments and a wave field synthesis thesis project using Linux and C++
Strong software craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD, pair programming, and low-latency system design
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — native-level Japanese is a non-negotiable hard requirement
No experience with embedded networking security technologies: MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, MAC authentication, Captive Portal, or device tamper detection
Primary technical stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React, cloud services) is entirely orthogonal to embedded firmware, Linux kernel development, and network switch engineering

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
